summary: Fase 6 (negocio cerrado / no-show business + auto-resolve 24h + $50 compensation) fully tested at service and endpoint level. 22/22 pytest cases pass covering strikes.issue_strike(pending_review), strikes.resolve_pending_strike (upheld/cleared/invalid), POST /bookings/{id}/no-show-business, POST /bookings/{id}/no-show-response, _process_no_show_report + process_expired_no_show_reports, GET/POST /admin/no-show-reports.

minor.issue: Spanish notification body hardcodes '$108.20' ('Te depositamos $108.20 + $50...') instead of the actual client_paid amount. Mostly cosmetic but misleads users when their payment differs. See bookings.py line ~1491.
Uses AuditAction.PAYMENT_RELEASE/PAYMENT_HOLD for no-show outcomes – slight misuse of audit taxonomy, consider adding NO_SHOW_RESOLVED* actions. Non-blocking.

When outcome='upheld' and severity later escalates (e.g., if 2 more strikes accrued while pending), the stored severity at issuance time is re-applied blindly. Edge case; acceptable for current flow but worth noting.

action_items: Consider parameterizing the refund amount in the client notification instead of the hardcoded $108.20.
(Optional) Add dedicated AuditAction entries for no-show dispute resolutions.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/bookings.py `_process_no_show_report` silently logs and continues when wallet credit or funds_state transitions fail – the flow still marks the booking cancelled and strike upheld even if the user was not actually refunded. Consider short-circuiting or producing a pending-reconcile record so failures do not cause silent data inconsistency.
Hardcoded compensation notification string '$108.20 + $50' leaks test data assumptions; replace with formatted dynamic values.

admin.py resolve no-show favor_business path only transitions 'disputed' -> 'available' but not back to 'pending_hold' if that was the prior state; for early-reported disputes the funds may be moved forward in the lifecycle unintentionally.
routers/bookings.py no-show endpoints use lazy inline `from services.X import ...` inside handlers; fine functionally but could cause minor import duplication – move to module top.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_F6_ prefixed bookings, transactions, wallet_transactions and strikes, all cleaned up in module teardown. No persistent seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fase 6 backend fully validated (22/22). Test file /app/backend/tests/test_fase6_no_show_business.py manipulates booking.date/time and no_show_report.auto_resolve_at directly in Mongo to simulate window edge cases and cron expiration. Frontend was intentionally skipped per request.
rca of the issue: N/A – no failing cases; only minor code-quality observations above.
